sections.paragraph:
i.q. Syr. ܕܥܶܟ to be extinguished, pr. of a lantern, or lamp, Pro 13:9, נֵר רְשָׁעִים יִדְעָךְ “the lamp of the wicked shall be put out,” i.e. their good fortune shall perish; compare the Arabic proverb, الدهر اطفا سراجى ill fortune has put out my lamp. Pro 20:20 24:20 Job 18:5, Job 18:6 21:17 . Applied to the destruction of enemies, Isa 43:17, and to the drying up of water, see Niphal.

Niphal, to become extinct, i.e. to dry up (when spoken of water), (comp. exstinguere aquam, Liv. v. 15; succum, Curt. vi. 4; mammas, Plin. xxiii. 2). Job 6:17.

Pual, to be extinct, applied to enemies, Psa 118:12.

[דָּעַל an unused root, see תִּדְעָל .]
